props.name: aria-controls
props.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current
element.

props.name: aria-current
props.description:
Indicates whether this element represents the current item within a container or set of related
elements.

props.name: aria-describedby
props.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

props.name: aria-details
props.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the
object.

props.name: aria-disabled
props.description: Indicates whether the element is disabled to users of assistive technology.

props.name: aria-expanded
props.description:
Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded
or collapsed.

props.name: aria-haspopup
props.description:
Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that
can be triggered by an element.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.

props.name: aria-labelledby
props.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

props.name: aria-pressed
props.description: Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

props.name: autoFocus
props.description: Whether the element should receive focus on render.

props.name: children
props.description:
The children of the component. A function may be provided to alter the children based on
component state.

props.name: excludeFromTabOrder
props.description:
Whether to exclude the element from the sequential tab order. If true,
the element will not be focusable via the keyboard by tabbing. This should
be avoided except in rare scenarios where an alternative means of accessing
the element or its functionality via the keyboard is available.

props.name: form
props.description:
The `<form>` element to associate the button with.
The value of this attribute must be the id of a `<form>` in the same document.
See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#form).

props.name: formAction
props.description:
The URL that processes the information submitted by the button.
Overrides the action attribute of the button's form owner.

props.name: formEncType
props.description: Indicates how to encode the form data that is submitted.

props.name: formMethod
props.description: Indicates the HTTP method used to submit the form.

props.name: formNoValidate
props.description: Indicates that the form is not to be validated when it is submitted.

props.name: formTarget
props.description: Overrides the target attribute of the button's form owner.

props.name: id
props.description:
The element's unique identifier. See
[MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/id).

props.name: isDisabled
props.description: Whether the button is disabled.

props.name: isPending
props.description:
Whether the button is in a pending state. This disables press and hover events
while retaining focusability, and announces the pending state to screen readers.

props.name: name
props.description: Submitted as a pair with the button's value as part of the form data.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Handler that is called when the element loses focus.

props.name: onClick
props.description:
**Not recommended – use `onPress` instead.** `onClick` is an alias for `onPress`
provided for compatibility with other libraries. `onPress` provides
additional event details for non-mouse interactions.

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Handler that is called when the element receives focus.

props.name: onFocusChange
props.description: Handler that is called when the element's focus status changes.

props.name: onHoverChange
props.description: Handler that is called when the hover state changes.

props.name: onHoverEnd
props.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction ends.

props.name: onHoverStart
props.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction starts.

props.name: onKeyDown
props.description: Handler that is called when a key is pressed.

props.name: onKeyUp
props.description: Handler that is called when a key is released.

props.name: onPress
props.description: Handler that is called when the press is released over the target.

props.name: onPressChange
props.description: Handler that is called when the press state changes.

props.name: onPressEnd
props.description:
Handler that is called when a press interaction ends, either
over the target or when the pointer leaves the target.

props.name: onPressStart
props.description: Handler that is called when a press interaction starts.

props.name: onPressUp
props.description:
Handler that is called when a press is released over the target, regardless of
whether it started on the target or not.

props.name: preventFocusOnPress
props.description:
Whether to prevent focus from moving to the button when pressing it.

Caution, this can make the button inaccessible and should only be used when alternative
keyboard interaction is provided, such as ComboBox's MenuTrigger or a NumberField's
increment/decrement control.

props.name: render
props.description:
Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function.
This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors
such as router links, animation libraries, and pre-styled components.

Requirements:

- You must render the expected element type (e.g. if `<button>` is expected, you cannot render an
  `<a>`).
- Only a single root DOM element can be rendered (no fragments).
- You must pass through props and ref to the underlying DOM element, merging with your own prop
  as appropriate.

props.name: slot
props.description:
A slot name for the component. Slots allow the component to receive props from a parent
component. An explicit `null` value indicates that the local props completely override all
props received from a parent.

props.name: type
props.description: The behavior of the button when used in an HTML form.

props.name: value
props.description: The value associated with the button's name when it's submitted with the form data.

props.name: aria-controls
props.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current
element.

props.name: aria-describedby
props.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

props.name: aria-details
props.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the
object.

props.name: aria-disabled
props.description: Indicates whether the element is disabled to users of assistive technology.

props.name: aria-expanded
props.description:
Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded
or collapsed.

props.name: aria-haspopup
props.description:
Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that
can be triggered by an element.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.

props.name: aria-labelledby
props.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

props.name: aria-pressed
props.description: Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

props.name: autoFocus
props.description: Whether the element should receive focus on render.

props.name: children
props.description:
The children of the component. A function may be provided to alter the children based on
component state.

props.name: defaultSelected
props.description: Whether the element should be selected (uncontrolled).

props.name: excludeFromTabOrder
props.description:
Whether to exclude the element from the sequential tab order. If true,
the element will not be focusable via the keyboard by tabbing. This should
be avoided except in rare scenarios where an alternative means of accessing
the element or its functionality via the keyboard is available.

props.name: id
props.description:
When used in a ToggleButtonGroup, an identifier for the item in `selectedKeys`. When used
standalone, a DOM id.

props.name: isDisabled
props.description: Whether the button is disabled.

props.name: isSelected
props.description: Whether the element should be selected (controlled).

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Handler that is called when the element loses focus.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Handler that is called when the element's selection state changes.

props.name: onClick
props.description:
**Not recommended – use `onPress` instead.** `onClick` is an alias for `onPress`
provided for compatibility with other libraries. `onPress` provides
additional event details for non-mouse interactions.

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Handler that is called when the element receives focus.

props.name: onFocusChange
props.description: Handler that is called when the element's focus status changes.

props.description: Handler that is called when the hover state changes.

props.name: onHoverEnd
props.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction ends.

props.name: onHoverStart
props.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction starts.

props.name: onKeyDown
props.description: Handler that is called when a key is pressed.

props.name: onKeyUp
props.description: Handler that is called when a key is released.

props.name: onPress
props.description: Handler that is called when the press is released over the target.

props.name: onPressChange
props.description: Handler that is called when the press state changes.

props.name: onPressEnd
props.description:
Handler that is called when a press interaction ends, either
over the target or when the pointer leaves the target.

props.name: onPressStart
props.description: Handler that is called when a press interaction starts.

props.name: onPressUp
props.description:
Handler that is called when a press is released over the target, regardless of
whether it started on the target or not.

Whether to prevent focus from moving to the button when pressing it.

Caution, this can make the button inaccessible and should only be used when alternative

Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function.
This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors
